characters.name: Jemima Puddle-Duck
characters.description: A white farmyard duck wearing a blue poke bonnet and a pink shawl. Earnest, naive, trusting, determined to hatch her own eggs.
characters.source: Jemima Puddle-Duck from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Jemima Puddle-Duck (1908)

characters.name: The Fox
characters.description: A sandy-whiskered gentleman fox. Wears a coat and reads a newspaper. Charming, polite, cunning — he plans to eat Jemima and her eggs. Called 'the gentleman with sandy whiskers' in the original.
characters.source: The Fox from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Jemima Puddle-Duck

characters.name: Kep
characters.description: A collie dog on the farm. Brown and white, intelligent, watchful. He is the one who sees through the fox's deception and rescues Jemima. Exercises true prudentia.
characters.source: Kep from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Jemima Puddle-Duck

characters.name: The Farmer's Wife
characters.description: A woman in a blue dress and white apron. Manages the farm and the poultry. She takes Jemima's eggs away to be hatched by hens, which frustrates Jemima.
characters.source: The Farmer's Wife from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Jemima Puddle-Duck

locations.name: The Farm
locations.description: An English farmyard with stone buildings, a barn, ducks, hens, and chicks. The farmer's wife tends the poultry.

locations.name: The Hill and Countryside
locations.description: Rolling green hills and open countryside surrounding the farm. Where Jemima flies to find a nesting spot.

locations.name: The Woods
locations.description: A shady wood with foxgloves and ferns. The fox's territory. He has a shed/hut made of sticks hidden among the trees.

locations.name: The Fox's Shed
locations.description: A tumbledown shed in the woods with a thatched roof. Inside is a bed of feathers — suspiciously soft. This is where Jemima lays her eggs.

pages.english_text: Jemima was a duck who lived on a farm.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_003.jpg] The farmyard scene: the farmer's wife at the door, Jemima among hens, ducks, and chicks in the yard. Establishes the setting.

pages.english_text: Jemima wanted to hatch her own eggs.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_004.jpg] Jemima in the barn looking frustrated, standing apart from the other hens. An egg visible nearby. She wants to sit on her eggs but is not allowed.

pages.english_text: But the farmer's wife always took the eggs away.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_005.jpg] The farmer's wife carrying eggs away while Jemima watches. Jemima looks upset — her eggs are being taken from her.

pages.english_text: So Jemima left the farm to find a secret nest.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_006.jpg] Jemima setting off from the farm, wearing her bonnet and shawl, walking determinedly along a path toward the hills.

pages.english_text: She flew over the hill, looking for a place.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_008.jpg] Jemima in flight, wings spread, bonnet on, soaring over green hills. A moment of freedom and determination.

pages.english_text: She landed in a wood full of foxgloves.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_010.jpg] Jemima standing in a shady clearing among tall foxglove flowers. A beautiful but ominous setting.

pages.english_text: A gentleman with sandy whiskers sat reading a newspaper.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_011.jpg] The fox sitting on a tree stump, reading a newspaper, looking elegant and refined. Jemima approaches. He looks friendly.

pages.english_text: Jemima told him all her troubles.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_012.jpg] Jemima talking earnestly to the fox, who listens with exaggerated sympathy. She is completely open and trusting.

pages.english_text: "I know a good place for a nest," said the fox.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_013.jpg] The fox leading Jemima to a tumbledown shed in the woods. He gestures toward it. The shed looks rough but Jemima looks hopeful.

pages.english_text: The shed was full of feathers. Very soft feathers.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_015.jpg] Jemima nestled contentedly in a deep bed of feathers inside the shed. She looks happy. The feathers are sinister — they belong to the fox's previous victims.

pages.english_text: Jemima laid nine eggs in the feathery nest.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_014.jpg] The fox politely closing the shed door, locking Jemima inside with her eggs. She does not realize the danger.

pages.english_text: The fox was very polite. He gave her food every day.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_016.jpg] The fox bowing politely to Jemima, who looks flattered. He is charming and attentive — but his motives are dark.

pages.english_text: He smiled at Jemima and asked about her eggs.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_017.jpg] The fox sitting very close to Jemima, head tilted, smiling. Too attentive. A predator being patient.

pages.english_text: Then the fox asked Jemima to bring herbs and onions.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_018.jpg] The fox standing by a gate, asking Jemima something. Jemima listens. He wants sage and onions — the stuffing for roast duck.

pages.english_text: Jemima gathered sage and onions for him. She did not understand.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_019.jpg] Jemima in the farm garden, gathering herbs in her shawl. She cheerfully collects the ingredients for her own roasting — without any suspicion.

pages.english_text: On the farm, Jemima met Kep the collie dog.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_020.jpg] Jemima talking to Kep the collie dog by the farmhouse door. She is telling him everything — where she goes, the kind gentleman.

pages.english_text: She told Kep everything. Kep listened carefully.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_021.jpg] Kep trotting off with purpose after hearing Jemima's story. He understands the danger immediately — the fox plans to eat Jemima.

pages.english_text: Jemima brought the herbs to the fox. He looked very happy.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_022.jpg] Jemima walking through the countryside carrying her bundle of herbs to the fox. She is innocent and cheerful, unaware of the danger.

pages.english_text: But Kep came with two foxhound dogs!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_024.jpg] Kep arriving with two foxhound dogs, running toward the fox's lair. They are coming to the rescue.

pages.english_text: The dogs chased the fox away! He ran into the woods.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_025.jpg] The foxhound dogs running and chasing. The fox is fleeing. A dramatic rescue scene.

pages.english_text: Jemima was safe, but the dogs had eaten her eggs.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_027.jpg] Jemima walking home sadly with Kep and the foxhounds. The farm visible in the background. Jemima is safe but heartbroken — her eggs are gone.

pages.english_text: Later, Jemima hatched four ducklings. Only four.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_028.jpg] Jemima standing proudly with four small yellow ducklings at the farm. A bittersweet happy ending — she has some ducklings, but fewer than she should have.
